name: Basil Ground Beef Spaghetti
ingredients:
2-3 lb ground beef
1 red bell pepper, diced
1 white onion, diced
1 bunch fresh basil, chopped
12 crimini mushrooms, chopped
1 jar (24 oz) tomato-based pasta sauce (garlic tomato, arrabbiata, or truffle marinara)
1 lb spaghetti
Salt and black pepper to taste
Red pepper flakes to taste
Grated parmesan to taste
directions:
Heat a large skillet or Dutch oven over medium heat. Add the diced onion and sauté for 5-6 minutes until softened and translucent.
Add the ground beef, season generously with salt and black pepper, and cook over medium-high heat for 8-10 minutes, breaking it up as it browns. Drain excess fat if needed.
Add red pepper flakes to taste and stir to combine.
Add the diced red bell pepper and cook for 4-5 minutes until slightly softened but still with a little bite.
Add the chopped mushrooms and cook for another 5-6 minutes until they release their moisture and begin to brown.
Pour in the jar of sauce, stir to combine, and reduce heat to low. Simmer uncovered for 15 minutes to meld the flavors.
Meanwhile, cook spaghetti in well-salted boiling water until al dente per package directions. Reserve a splash of pasta water before draining.
Stir the fresh basil into the sauce just before serving.
Serve the meat sauce over spaghetti, topped with grated parmesan.
notes: If the sauce thickens too much during simmering, loosen it with a splash of the reserved pasta water. A truffle marinara makes this feel a bit more elevated — worth trying.
